headline: Amazon aims to raise $25 billion from bond sale, Bloomberg News reports
summary: Amazon.com is looking to raise at least $25 billion through a U.S. dollar bond sale, Bloomberg ‌News reported on Tuesday, in the company's latest push ‌to fund its hefty AI investments.  Tech companies have been tapping debt markets and ​launching equity sales to fund their costly AI infrastructure build-out.  Big Tech, including Amazon, Alphabet, Microsoft and Meta, are expected to spend more than $700 billion on AI this year.

Tech companies have been tapping debt markets and ​launching equity sales to fund their costly AI infrastructure build-out.  Big Tech, including Amazon, Alphabet, Microsoft and Meta, are expected to spend more than $700 billion on AI this year.

headline: Tech Investors Take a Fresh Look at AI Investments
summary: Tech investors are reassessing their investments in artificial intelligence, despite the technology being a positive long-term driver, ING's Jan Frederik Slijkerman wrote in a report.  Technology companies' revenues and Ebitda are set to rise, but investors remain nervous, he said.  Investors might experience a slowdown in earnings-per-share growth and a decline in valuation multiples as infrastructure spending leads to higher depreciation costs and reduced share buybacks, Slijkerman added.

content.title: Tech Investors Take a Fresh Look at AI Investments
content.summary: Tech investors are reassessing their investments in artificial intelligence, despite the technology being a positive long-term driver, ING's Jan Frederik Slijkerman wrote in a report.  Technology companies' revenues and Ebitda are set to rise, but investors remain nervous, he said.  Investors might experience a slowdown in earnings-per-share growth and a decline in valuation multiples as infrastructure spending leads to higher depreciation costs and reduced share buybacks, Slijkerman added.

headline: Is Google Cloud Officially Driving the Train?
summary: Let’s be honest. When a stock you own doubles, you feel like a genius. When it’s a behemoth like Alphabet (GOOGL), and it returns +105% in a year, you might wonder if you’ve misread the chart. You haven’t. Over the past 12 months, while the S&P 500 gained a respectable 21%, Alphabet left it in the dust. It also trounced its mega-cap peers; Microsoft (MSFT) stock returned -21.9%, and Meta Platforms (META) returned -16.2% over the same period.

content.title: Is Google Cloud Officially Driving the Train?

When it’s a behemoth like Alphabet (GOOGL), and it returns +105% in a year, you might wonder if you’ve misread the chart. You haven’t. Over the past 12 months, while the S&P 500 gained a respectable 21%, Alphabet left it in the dust. It also trounced its mega-cap peers; Microsoft (MSFT) stock returned -21.9%, and Meta Platforms (META) returned -16.2% over the same period.

content.title: Microsoft edges higher as in-house AI push takes aim at copilot costs
content.summary: Investing.com -- Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) shares ticked up 1.75% on Tuesday, outperforming a sluggish Nasdaq following reports that the tech giant is beginning to ditch pricey third-party AI models in favor of its own homegrown technology. According to Bloomberg, Microsoft has quietly deployed its newly minted, internally developed MAI models directly into Excel and Outlook. headline: Amazon aims to raise $25 billion from bond sale, source says
summary: Amazon.com is looking to raise at least $25 billion through a U.S. dollar bond sale, ‌a source familiar with the matter told Reuters on Tuesday, in the ‌company's latest push to fund its hefty AI investments.  Tech companies have been tapping debt markets and launching ​equity sales to fund their costly AI infrastructure build-out.  Big Tech, including Amazon, Alphabet, Microsoft and Meta, are expected to spend more than $700 billion on AI this year.

content.summary: Amazon.com is looking to raise at least $25 billion through a U.S. dollar bond sale, ‌a source familiar with the matter told Reuters on Tuesday, in the ‌company's latest push to fund its hefty AI investments.  Tech companies have been tapping debt markets and launching ​equity sales to fund their costly AI infrastructure build-out.  Big Tech, including Amazon, Alphabet, Microsoft and Meta, are expected to spend more than $700 billion on AI this year.

headline: Who will be the AI trade's real winners: The makers vs. the takers?
summary: Samsung Electronics (005930.KS) released preliminary second quarter earnings results on Tuesday, reporting a 19-fold profit jump from a year ago, amounting to $58 billion. Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman welcomes Yahoo Finance Senior Business Reporter Ines Ferré and Interactive Brokers chief strategist Steve Sosnick to help break down whether Samsung's breakout numbers still weren't enough to impress Wall Street investors and what it signifies for the broader chip space.

content.title: Who will be the AI trade's real winners: The makers vs. the takers?

Samsung Electronics (005930.KS) released preliminary second quarter earnings results on Tuesday, reporting a 19-fold profit jump from a year ago, amounting to $58 billion.

Morning Brief Host Julie Hyman welcomes Yahoo Finance Senior Business Reporter Ines Ferré and Interactive Brokers chief strategist Steve Sosnick to help break down whether Samsung's breakout numbers still weren't enough to impress Wall Street investors and what it signifies for the broader chip space.

headline: The Dividend Growth Snowball: How Modest Income Today Can Become Serious Income Later
summary: A 12% yield looks unbeatable on day one. A retiree who wants $60,000 a year needs only about $500,000 at that yield, compared with roughly $1.7 million at a 3.5% yield. But retirement income is not a one-year problem. The better question is which income stream can hold up after inflation, market cycles, and years ... The Dividend Growth Snowball: How Modest Income Today Can Become Serious Income Later

content.title: The Dividend Growth Snowball: How Modest Income Today Can Become Serious Income Later
content.summary: A 12% yield looks unbeatable on day one. A retiree who wants $60,000 a year needs only about $500,000 at that yield, compared with roughly $1.7 million at a 3.5% yield. But retirement income is not a one-year problem. The better question is which income stream can hold up after inflation, market cycles, and years ... headline: Microsoft (MSFT) Faces Two New Class Actions Over Copilot And Data Center Noise
summary: Microsoft faces a new securities fraud class action alleging misleading statements about Copilot and Azure performance. A separate class action from residents near the Mount Pleasant, Wisconsin data center claims harmful noise pollution. Both lawsuits introduce fresh legal and reputational questions around Microsoft’s AI rollout and data center expansion. These cases arrive at a time when Microsoft (NasdaqGS:MSFT) is closely watched for its AI push and cloud position, with the stock last...

headline: Amazon Stocks Climbs As AI Push Drives $25 Billion Bond Sale
summary: Amazon stock climbed Tuesday, following reports the tech giant is raising at least $25 billion through a bond offering to support its big investments into AI-related infrastructure.  The Seattle-based Amazon is raising the funds through an eight-part bond sale, according to reports in Bloomberg and the Wall Street Journal.  Amazon disclosed plans for the bond sale in regulatory filings Tuesday but did not disclose the amount.

content.title: Amazon Stocks Climbs As AI Push Drives $25 Billion Bond Sale
content.summary: Amazon stock climbed Tuesday, following reports the tech giant is raising at least $25 billion through a bond offering to support its big investments into AI-related infrastructure.  The Seattle-based Amazon is raising the funds through an eight-part bond sale, according to reports in Bloomberg and the Wall Street Journal.  Amazon disclosed plans for the bond sale in regulatory filings Tuesday but did not disclose the amount.

headline: Microsoft Says Xbox Isn’t For Sale. Why It Should Be.
summary: Microsoft  is a massive company with hundreds of billions in revenue from dominant businesses.  Analysts expect that  Microsoft’s  gaming revenue, which includes software sales and Xbox hardware, shrunk 7.5% in the just ended fiscal year, to $21.7 billion, according to Visible Alpha.  Before the AI boom, gaming was a significant part of Microsoft’s growth story, says D.A. Davidson analyst Gil Luria.

content.title: Microsoft Says Xbox Isn’t For Sale. Why It Should Be.
content.summary: Microsoft  is a massive company with hundreds of billions in revenue from dominant businesses.  Analysts expect that  Microsoft’s  gaming revenue, which includes software sales and Xbox hardware, shrunk 7.5% in the just ended fiscal year, to $21.7 billion, according to Visible Alpha.  Before the AI boom, gaming was a significant part of Microsoft’s growth story, says D.A. Davidson analyst Gil Luria.
